title: 1RM Bench Press Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate your bench press one-rep max using the Brzycki formula. Free strength training calculator.
intro: Strength training relies on key physiological principles: progressive overload, adaptation, and recovery. Intensity zones, weekly volume, and rest periods determine your performance gains. This free bench press 1RM calculator uses the Brzycki formula to estimate your maximum lift based on your working weight and reps. Updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current with proven training science.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **1RM = weight × (1 + reps ÷ 30)**. Enter your lift weight and rep count to instantly estimate your one-rep max. Based on the evidence-backed Brzycki method.
useCases: Estimate your maximum bench press strength
Learning and teaching strength training principles
explanation:
**Strength training** combines scientific knowledge (physiology, biomechanics) with practical experience. Intensity zones, progressive overload, adequate rest, and proper nutrition form the complete training system.

## How the Calculator Works

This calculator applies the Brzycki formula to estimate your one-rep max. It verifies your inputs against typical training ranges and alerts you to values outside normal parameters.

The Brzycki formula is reliable for 1–10 rep ranges and becomes less accurate beyond 10 reps. The more reps you perform, the less precise the estimate becomes.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an estimate for reference only. For critical decisions (medical, training program overhaul, injury recovery), consult with a qualified strength coach, physical therapist, or physician. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: 1RM = weight × (1 + reps ÷ 30). It estimates your one-rep max from a submaximal lift. More reps = lower accuracy.
Very accurate for 1–10 reps. Beyond 10 reps, accuracy decreases. Use it as a training guide, not an absolute measure.
A direct 1RM test gives exact numbers but carries injury risk. Use this calculator to program training safely first.

faq.q: How do I improve my bench press?
faq.a: Progressive overload, moderate volume (6–12 reps), sufficient rest (48–72 hrs between sessions), and proper form.

faq.q: What's a good warm-up for bench press?
faq.a: Use an ascending pyramid: light weight for 5 reps, then gradually increase weight with 2–3 minute rest between sets.
Yes. All our calculators are completely free and require no sign-up or account.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send your data to any server.

faq.q: Can I use this for other lifts?
faq.a: Yes. The Brzycki formula works for any barbell exercise (squat, deadlift, overhead press, etc.).

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
